reason: active يَقْتُلُونَ 'they kill' vs passive يُقْتَلُونَ 'they are killed' collide under norm_strict; voice is per-loc, not one surface gloss
and-they-kill (active) vs and-they-are-killed (passive); voice varies by āyah
بَنِين 'sons' is the broken plural of ٱبْن, a DIFFERENT lexeme from root ب-ن-ي 'to build'; never a verb gloss
'with the sons' — noun, must not be repaired onto the build-root entry
noun 'thirst'; the ظ-م-أ entry gloss is verbal ('to be thirsty') — a verb gloss on a noun is a POS mismatch; the form is already stored
imperative verb اهْدِ + نا OBJECT pronoun ('guide us'); نا is not a possessive — a verb's enclitic is object/subject
verb يَأْمُرُ + كُمْ OBJECT pronoun ('He commands you'); not a possessed noun
و + إِنَّ (emphatic particle 'indeed', distinct from conditional إِن 'if') + ها; a particle bundle, not stem authoring
ف + لِ (preposition 'for') + هُمْ ('so for them'); preposition+pronoun, not a possessed-noun host
Form I كَذَبَ 'lied' vs Form II كَذَّبَ 'denied/rejected' vs passive كُذِبُوا collide under norm_strict; form+voice are per-loc
ٱلْمُلْك 'dominion' vs ٱلْمَلِك 'king' vs مَلَك 'angel' share the norm_strict key الملك; harakāt/sense are per-loc
common adjective 'righteous' vs the Prophet Ṣāliḥ (proper noun); referent-sensitive, never propagate the name onto the adjective
يَدْعُونَ 'they call' vs يُدْعَوْنَ 'they are summoned' vs Form VIII يَدَّعُونَ 'they claim'; form+voice per-loc
تَوَلَّوْا 'they turned away' (Form V perfect) vs تُوَلُّوا 'you turn' — form/person/voice per-loc
يَنظُرُونَ 'they look/wait' vs يُنظَرُونَ 'they are reprieved' (passive Form IV sense); voice per-loc
Form I يَكْفُرُ 'disbelieves' vs Form II يُكَفِّرُ 'expiates' vs passive يُكْفَرُ; form/voice per-loc, opposite senses
